# Collect Microsoft 365 logs

**Source:** https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/ingestion/default-parsers/office-365/  
**Scraped:** 2026-03-05T09:17:36.291018Z

---

Home
Documentation
Security
Google Security Operations
Guides
Stay organized with collections
Save and categorize content based on your preferences.
Collect Microsoft 365 logs
Supported in:
Google secops
SIEM
This document describes how you can collect Microsoft 365 logs by setting up a Google Security Operations
feed and how log fields map to Google Security Operations Unified Data Model (UDM) fields.
This document also lists the supported audited activities and supported Microsoft 365 version.
For an overview about data ingestion to Google Security Operations, see
Data ingestion to Google Security Operations
.
Overview
The following deployment architecture diagram shows how Microsoft 365 and Google Security Operations feed
is configured to send logs to Google Security Operations. Each customer deployment might
differ from this representation and might be more complex.
The architecture diagram shows the following components:
Microsoft 365
. The Microsoft 365 service from which you collect logs.
Google Security Operations feed
. The Google Security Operations feed that fetches logs from Microsoft 365 and writes logs to Google Security Operations.
Google Security Operations
. Google Security Operations retains and analyzes the logs from
Microsoft 365.
An ingestion label identifies the parser which normalizes raw log data
to structured UDM format. The information in this document applies to the parser
with the
OFFICE_365
ingestion label.
Before you begin
Ensure you have the following prerequisites:
All systems in the deployment architecture are configured with the UTC time zone.
Microsoft 365 version 2204 Build 16.0.15128.20248 or later
A
Microsoft 365 Enterprise E5 subscription
with Microsoft Security and Compliance Center feature
Deployment Overview
A typical deployment consists of Microsoft Office 365 which sends the logs, and the Google SecOps feed which fetches the logs.
How to configure Microsoft 365 logs
Grant the required privileges and permissions to the user to generate and export
different events for all the supported Microsoft products. A user whose credentials
are used to authenticate against the API must have the
ActivityFeed.Read
permission.
To ingest DLP data, the
ActivityFeed.ReadDlp
permission is required.
For information about permissions, see
Permissions to access management APIs
Configure Microsoft 365 to search and export logs. Microsoft Entra ID
is the directory service for Microsoft 365. It takes up to 24 hours to generate the logs.
For more information, see
Search the audit log
Microsoft Office 365 logs collection in Google SecOps is done via third-party API feeds integration. To configure it, you need to create and configure a Microsoft Entra App that Google SecOps will use. Follow these steps to create and configure the Entra ID App:
Set up the Microsoft Entra ID app
Configure the Microsoft Entra for permissions
Create client secret in the Entra app
Note:
For the Google SecOps feed, Entra App needs to have Application-level permissions (not Delegated permissions):
Grant the required privileges and permissions to the user to generate and export different events for all the supported Microsoft products.
A user whose credentials are used to authenticate against the API must have the ActivityFeed.Read permission.
To ingest DLP data, the ActivityFeed.ReadDlp permission is required.
For information about permissions, see
Permissions to access management APIs
Note down the created Entra App Client Secret, Entra App Client ID, Entra ID Tenant ID.
The created client secret value is displayed only at the time of creation.
Set up feeds
There are two different entry points to set up feeds in the
Google SecOps platform:
SIEM Settings
>
Feeds
>
Add New Feed
Content Hub
>
Content Packs
>
Get Started
Set up feeds from SIEM Settings
>
Feeds
Click the
Azure platform
pack.
Locate the
Office 365
feed.
Specify values for the following fields:
Source Type
: Third party API (recommended)
OAUTH client ID
: Specify the client ID that you obtained previously.
OAUTH client secret
: Specify the client secret that you obtained previously.
Tenant ID
: Specify the tenant ID that you obtained previously.
Content Type
: Specify type from the following options.
Audit.AzureActiveDirectory: User identity management.
Audit.Exchange: Mail and calendaring server.
Audit.SharePoint: Web-based collaborative platform.
Audit.General: Includes all other workloads not included in the previous content types.
DLP.All: Data loss prevention workloads.
API Full path
: Microsoft Graph REST API endpoint URL.
API Authentication Endpoint
: Microsoft Active Directory Authentication Endpoint.
Advanced Options
Feed Name
: A prepopulated value that identifies the feed.
Asset Namespace
:
Namespace associated with the feed
.
Ingestion Labels
: Labels applied to all events from this feed.
Click
Create feed
.
For more information about Google Security Operations feeds, see
Google Security Operations feeds documentation
.
Supported Activities and Products for the Google Security Operations
Review the activities and products that the Google Security Operations parser supports.
The following table list the activities and products that the Google Security Operations parser supports:

Products
File and page activities
SharePoint Online and OneDrive for Business
Folder activities
SharePoint Online and OneDrive for Business
SharePoint list activities
SharePoint Online
Sharing and access request activities

SharePoint Online
Site administration activities
SharePoint Online
Exchange mailbox activities
Microsoft 365 Group mailboxes
User administration activities
Microsoft 365 admin center
Azure AD group administration activities
Microsoft 365 admin center
Application administration activities
When an administrator adds or changes an application that is registered in Azure AD
Role administration activities
Microsoft 365 admin center
Directory administration activities
Microsoft 365 admin center
Power BI activities
Power BI
Microsoft Teams activities
Microsoft Teams

Shifts app in Microsoft Teams
Microsoft Teams Healthcare activities
Patients application in Microsoft Teams
Microsoft Teams Shifts activities
Shifts app in Microsoft Teams
Yammer activities
Yammer
Microsoft Power Automate activities
Power Automate (formerly called Microsoft Flow)
Microsoft PowerApps activities
Power Apps
Microsoft Stream activities
Microsoft Stream
Quarantine activities
Quarantine email messages in Office 365
Microsoft Forms activities
Microsoft Teams
Sensitivity label activities
Labeling activities for SharePoint Online and Teams
Retention policy and retention label activities
NA
Briefing email activities
Briefing email
MyAnalytics activities
MyAnalytics
Information barriers activities
NA
Disposition review activities
NA
Communication compliance activities
NA
Undefined Activity
NA
Supported Microsoft 365 log formats
The Microsoft 365 parser supports logs in JSON format.
Supported Microsoft 365 Sample Logs

Field mapping reference
This section explains how the Google Security Operations parser maps Microsoft 365 log fields to Google Security Operations Unified Data Model (UDM) fields for the supported operations and workloads.
Common fields
The following table lists the common log fields and their corresponding UDM fields.
Common log field
UDM field
Logic
AgentType
principal.resource.attribute.labels[agent_type]
ForAllUsers
target.resource.attribute.labels[for_all_users]
IsHiddenList
target.resource.attribute.labels[is_hidden_list]
IsDocLib
target.resource.attribute.labels[is_doc_lib]
CustomEvent
additional.fields[CustomEvent]
HostAppId
additional.fields[HostAppId]
ActorIP
principal.ip
CloudInstanceName
principal.resource.attribute.labels[cloud_instance_name]
AuthType
additional.fields[AuthType]
TokenType
additional.fields[TokenType]
CrossMailboxOperations
additional.fields[CrossMailboxOperations]
MbxLoginLocalQueueADLookupInfo.Puid
principal.user.attribute.labels[MbxLoginLocalQueueADLookupInfo_Puid]
MbxLoginLocalQueueADLookupInfo.OrgIdPuid
principal.group.product_object_id
MbxLoginLocalQueueADLookupInfo.Smtp
principal.user.email_addresses
MbxLoginLocalQueueADLookupInfo.SearchScope
additional.fields[MbxLoginLocalQueueADLookupInfo_SearchScope]
MbxLoginLocalQueueADLookupInfo.ConsumerMailbox
principal.user.attribute.labels[MbxLoginLocalQueueADLookupInfo_ConsumerMailbox]
AttachmentId
about.resource.attribute.labels[attachment_id]
AttachmentSizeInBytes
about.resource.attribute.labels[attachment_size_in_bytes]

additional.fields[AggregateDurationInSeconds]

EndpointMetaData.SensitiveInformation.Confidence
security_result.confidence
Iterate for each element
s_info
in the
EndpointMetaData.SensitiveInformation
log field array, then
If the
s_info.Confidence
log field value is greater than
80
then, the
security_result.confidence
UDM field is set to
HIGH_CONFIDENCE
.
Else, if
s_info.Confidence
log field value is greater than
50
then, the
security_result.confidence
UDM field is set to
MEDIUM_CONFIDENCE
.
Else,
security_result.confidence
UDM field is set to
LOW_CONFIDENCE
.

security_result.confidence

c_info

log field array, then
If the
c_info.Confidence
log field value is greater than
80

.

c_info.Confidence
log field value is greater than
50
then, the
security_result.confidence
UDM field is set to
MEDIUM_CONFIDENCE
.
Else,
security_result.confidence
UDM field is set to
LOW_CONFIDENCE
.

ID
metadata.product_log_id
RecordType
security_result.detection_fields.key/value
security_result.detection_fields.key is set to {RecordeType} - RecordTypeNameFromDoc
security_result.detection_fields.value is set to RecordTypeDescriptionFromDoc
CreationTime
metadata.event_timestamp
Operation
metadata.product_event_type
OrganizationId
principal.resource.product_object_id
ActorInfoString
network.http.user_agent
If the
ActorInfoString
log field value is not empty and if it matches the regular expression pattern
*?UserAgent=(?
[^;]+).*
then, the
temp_agent
extracted field is mapped to the
network.http.user_agent
UDM field.
If
ActorInfoString
log field value contains substring as
UserAgent=NoUserAgent
and if
ClientInfoString
log field value matches the regular expression pattern
Client=.*;(?<agent>[^;]+).*\\[AppId=
then, the
agent
extracted field is mapped to the
network.http.user_agent
UDM field.
Else it will map empty string.
If
ActorInfoString
log field value contains substring as
Client=
and if it matches the regular expression pattern
Client=.*;(?
[^;]+).*\\[AppId=
then, the
temp_agent
extracted field is mapped to the
network.http.user_agent
UDM field.
If
ActorInfoString
doesn't matches any regular expression pattern, then the
ActorInfoString
log field value is mapped to the
network.http.user_agent
UDM field.
If
ActorInfoString
log field value is empty, then the
ClientInfoString
log field value is mapped to the
network.http.user_agent
UDM field.
UserType
principal.user.attribute.roles.name
UserId
principal.user.email_addresses or principal.user.userid
target.user.email_addresses or target.user.userid
If is Operation is UserLoggedIn, UserLoginFailed, Add OAuth2PermissionGrant, TeamsUserSignedOut, or Add delegated
  permission grant then UserId is mapped to target.user
  else UserId is mapped to principal.user
If UserId value contains email address then it is mapped to email_address, else it is mapped to userid.
ClientIP
principal.ip and principal.port
Workload
target.application
AppAccessContext
network.session.id
security_result.detection_fields.key/value
AADSessionId is mapped to network.session.id
CorrelationId is mapped to security_result.detection_fields.key/value

SourceWorkload
target.resource.attribute.labels[source_workload]
If the
SourceWorkload
log field value is equal to
0
then, the
target.resource.attribute.labels[source_workload]
UDM field is set to
SharePoint Online
.
Else, if
SourceWorkload
log field value is equal to
1
then, the
target.resource.attribute.labels[source_workload]
UDM field is set to
OneDrive for Business
.
Else, if
SourceWorkload
log field value is equal to
3
then, the
target.resource.attribute.labels[source_workload]
UDM field is set to
Microsoft Teams
.
AuthenticationStatus
additional.fields[authentication_status]
If the
AuthenticationStatus
log field value is equal to
0
then, the
additional.fields[authentication_status]
UDM field is set to
Success
.
Else, if
AuthenticationStatus
log field value is equal to
1
then, the
additional.fields[authentication_status]
UDM field is set to
Failure
.
UserTrainingEvent
principal.user.attribute.labels[user_training_event]
If the
UserTrainingEvent
log field value is equal to
1
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels[user_training_event]
UDM field is set to
TrainingAssigned
.
Else, if
UserTrainingEvent
log field value is equal to
2
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels[user_training_event]
UDM field is set to
TrainingUpdated
.
Else, if
UserTrainingEvent
log field value is equal to
3
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels[user_training_event]
UDM field is set to
TrainingCompleted
.
Else, if
UserTrainingEvent
log field value is equal to
4
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels[user_training_event]
UDM field is set to
TrainingPreviouslyAssigned
.
Else, if
UserTrainingEvent
log field value is equal to
5
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels[user_training_event]
UDM field is set to
TrainingNotCompleted
.

ClientViewSignaled
The following table lists the log fields and corresponding UDM mappings for the operation "ClientViewSignaled" and workload "SharePoint" or "OneDrive":
Log field
UDM mapping
metadata.event_type is mapped to USER_RESOURCE_UPDATE_CONTENT
target.resource.resource_type is set to STORAGE_OBJECT
ObjectId is mapped to target.url
NOTE: Because ClientViewSignaled events are signaled by the client, rather than the server, it's possible the event may not be logged by the server and therefore may not appear in the audit log. It's also possible that information in the audit record may not be trustworthy. However, because the user's identity is validated by the token used to create the signal, the user's identity listed in the corresponding audit record is accurate.

principal.platform
principal.hostname
If Name is OS {
If Value is match to Windows then principal.platform is WINDOWS
If Value is match to Mac then principal_plateform is MAC
if Value is match to Linux then principal_plateform is LINUX
}
If Name is SessionId then Value is mapped to network.session_id
If Name is OS then Value is mapped to principal.platform
If Name is DisplayName then Value is mapped to principal.hostname

AzureActiveDirectoryEventType
target.resource.attribute.labels.key/value
ExtendedProperties
network.http.user_agent
extensions.auth.type
extensions.auth.mechanism
If Name is RequestType and Value is match to Saml.* or OAuth2.* then extensions.auth.type is mapped to MACHINE
If Name is RequestType and Value is match to Login.* then extensions.auth.type is mapped to REMOTE_INTERACTIVE
If Name is UserAgent then Value is mapped to network.http.user_agent
If Name is UserAuthenticationMethod then Based on Value it will map with extensions.auth.type
If Name is requestType then Based on Value it will map with extensions.auth.type

ModifiedProperties
target.platform
target.ptatform_version
security_result.description
target.resource.name
security_result.summaryIf DisplayName value present in ModifiedProperties field then we will map DisplayName with target.resource.name otherwise map ID of Target field if Type is 1.
If Name is DeviceOSType then NewValue is mapped to target.platform
If Name =DeviceOSVersion then NewValue is mapped to target.ptatform_version
If Name is DevicePhysicalIds then NewValue is mapped to security_result.description
If Name is DisplayName then NewVale is mapped to target.resource.name
If Name is Included Updated Properties then NewValue is mapped to security_result.summary

UDM mapping
metadata.event_type is mapped to SETTING_CREATION
target.resource.resource_type is set to SETTING
Required fields for SETTING_CREATION UDM validation : principal.machineid (IP or hostname or assetId or mac etc).
ClientIP field is mandatory field for all the office 365 activities as per official doc of Office 365, but in some cases ClientIP field is absent
If ClientIP field is absent then metadata.event_type is mapped to GENERIC_EVENT.

Update
The following table lists the log fields and corresponding UDM mappings for the operation "Update" and workload "Exchange":
Log field
UDM mapping
metadata.event_type is mapped to EMAIL_UNCATEGORIZED
LogonType
If the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
2
, then the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
INTERACTIVE
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
3
or
8
, then the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
NETWORK
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
4
, then the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
BATCH
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
5
, then the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
SERVICE
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
7
, then the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
UNLOCK
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
9
, then the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
NEW_CREDENTIALS
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
9
, then the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
REMOTE_INTERACTIVE
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
9
, then the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
CACHED_INTERACTIVE
.
Else, the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
MECHANISM_UNSPECIFIED
.

Parameters
network.application_protocol
target.user.email_addresses
target.group.email_addresses
If Name is EmailAddresses then extract value of Value, then emails and mail_key from that Value field, email_addresses with target.user.email_addresses, mail_key with network.email.mail_id
If Name is ExternalEmailAddress then extract value of Value field, then extract protocol and emails from it, map protocol with network.application_protocol and emails with target.group.email_addresses.
Protocol is mapped to network.application_protocol
EmailAddresses is mapped to target.user.email_addresses
ExternalEmailAddress is mapped to target.group.email_addresses

UDM mapping
metadata.event_type is mapped to SETTING_MODIFICATION
target.resource.resource_type is set to SETTING
Required fields for SETTING_MODIFICATION UDM validation : principal.machineid (IP or hostname or assetId or mac etc).
ClientIP field is mandatory field for all the office 365 activities as per official doc of Office 365, but in some cases ClientIP field is absent
If ClientIP field is absent then metadata.event_type is mapped to GENERIC_EVENT.

UDM mapping
metadata.event_type is mapped to GROUP_MODIFICATION
If ClientIP field is absent then metadata.event_type is mapped to GENERIC_EVENT.
if ResultStatus is TRUE then
security_result.action is set to ALLOW
else
security_result.action is set to BLOCK

OriginatingServer
principal.hostname
Parameters
network.application_protocol
target.user.email_addresses
target.group.email_addresses
If Name is EmailAddresses then extract value of Value, then emails and mail_key from that Value field, email_addresses with target.user.email_addresses, mail_key with network.email.mail_id
If Name is ExternalEmailAddress then extract value of Value field, then extract protocol and emails from it, map protocol with network.application_protocol and emails with target.group.email_addresses.
Protocol is mapped to network.application_protocol
EmailAddresses is mapped to target.user.email_addresses
ExternalEmailAddress is mapped to target.group.email_addresses
SessionId
network.session_id

.

LogonType
extensions.auth.mechanism
If the
LogonType
log field value is not
empty
then, If the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
2
then, the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
INTERACTIVE
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
3
or the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
8
then, the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
NETWORK
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
4
then, the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
BATCH
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
5
then, the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
SERVICE
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
7
then, the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
UNLOCK
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
9
then, the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
NEW_CREDENTIALS
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
10
then, the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
REMOTE_INTERACTIVE
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
11
then, the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
CACHED_INTERACTIVE
.
Else, the
extensions.auth.mechanism
UDM field is set to
MECHANISM_UNSPECIFIED
.
principal.user.attribute.labels[logon_type]
If the
LogonType
log field value is not
empty
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels.key
UDM field is set to
logon_type
and If the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
0
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels.value
UDM field is set to
Owner
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
1
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels.value
UDM field is set to
Admin
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
2
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels.value
UDM field is set to
Delegated
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
3
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels.value
UDM field is set to
Transport
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
4
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels.value
UDM field is set to
SystemService
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
5
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels.value
UDM field is set to
BestAccess
.
Else, if the
LogonType
log field value is equal to
6
then, the
principal.user.attribute.labels.value
UDM field is set to
DelegatedAdmin
.
P1Sender
principal.user.email_addresses
network.email.reply_to
network.smtp.mail_from
If the
Operation
log field value is one of the following:
UserSubmission
UserSubmissionTriage
AirInvestigationData
then, the
P1Sender
log field is mapped to the
network.smtp.mail_from
UDM field.
Else, the
P1Sender
log field is mapped to the
network.email.reply_to
UDM field.

and map
member

Parameters
target.user.email_addresses
Iterate through
Parameters
: If
field.Name
is
BlockedSendersAndDomains
, iterate through
field.Value
, mapping valid emails to
target.user.email_addresses
.
about.user.email_addresses
Iterate through
Parameters
: If
field.Name
is equal to
BlockedSendersAndDomains
and
field.Value
is not
empty
, iterate through each
email
in
field.Value
. If
email
matches the regular expression pattern
(^.+@.+$)
, the
email
is mapped to the
about.user.email_addresses
UDM field.

Parameters
Iterate through
Parameters
for
Name
,
ManagedBy
, and
Members
, mapping to various
target.group
and
target.user
fields.
target.group.group_display_name
,
about.user.attribute.labels
,
about.user.user_display_name
Iterate through each
parameter
in
Parameters
:
If
parameter.Name
is equal to
Name
, the
parameter.Value
is mapped to the
target.group.group_display_name
UDM field.
Else if
parameter.Name
is equal to
ManagedBy
, the
parameter.Value
is mapped to
about.user.attribute.labels.value
and
about.user.attribute.labels.key
is set to
managed_by
.
Else if
parameter.Name
is equal to
Members
, the
parameter.Value
is mapped to the
about.user.user_display_name
UDM field.
